content:
AUDIENCE:
This reader is an 8-year-old child who is naturally curious about the world around them. They likely know some basic facts about the Earth, weather, and perhaps that the planet is getting warmer, but they don’t understand complicated science terms or processes. They care about animals, nature, and their own future, but get bored or confused by too many big words or long explanations. They enjoy simple stories and clear examples that relate to things they see or experience. A friendly, encouraging, and easy-to-understand tone will help keep their interest.

===BEGIN===
Have you ever felt like a big cozy blanket is wrapped around you? Well, the Earth has a special blanket too, called greenhouse gases. These gases are like invisible air that keeps our planet warm enough for people, animals, and plants to live.

The sun sends light and heat to Earth. Some of the heat bounces back into space, but the greenhouse gases catch some of it and hold it close, just like a blanket keeps you warm. This is good because if Earth got too cold, we couldn’t live here!

But now, there are too many greenhouse gases because people burn things like coal, oil, and gas for energy. This makes the blanket thicker and warmer, so the Earth is getting hotter — that’s called global warming. This can make weather change and harm animals and plants.

We can help by using cleaner energy and taking care of nature to keep our Earth comfy and happy!
===END===
